response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r1c4 is in acute energy crisis: islanded with SoC at 33% (0.99 kWh usable) while sustaining a 1.0 kW load that will breach the 0.30 kWh minimum operating reserve in ~41 minutes. The memory log reveals a sustained barrage of requests from r1c5 (SoC ~10%) and r2c4 (SoC ~39–42%), both below sharing thresholds, while r1c4 itself has been rejected by upstream peers (r0c4, r1c3) citing cluster-wide deficit and self-preservation logic. The current conservative policy correctly prioritizes survival: no normal shares are sustainable. However, the situation has deteriorated to the point where load shedding and urgent grid-reconnection signaling are now the only viable paths. Continuing to reject all requests while load remains at 1.0 kW will result in brownout within the hour. The policy must remain defensive but signal critical urgency to the coordination layer.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.70
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.01
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded cluster-wide deficit; r1c4 SoC 0.33/3 kWh declining toward minimum reserve (0.30 kWh). Load 1.0 kW unsustainable without external supply or immediate load shedding. No recovery above 0.60 kWh threshold observed. Reject all normal requests. Emergency shares (0.01 kW/tick max) only if own SoC exceeds 0.70 kWh (currently impossible). Prioritize grid reconnection signal and load shedding urgently."
ttl_ticks: 2
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
